The 4 biggest hiring mistakes biopharma executives keep making
snippet: Finding the right people for critical open roles can be difficult even for biopharma leaders. In this column, Kaye/Bassman’s Michael Pietrack discusses four pitfalls executives face during the hiring process, starting with confusing scientific brilliance with leadership ability.

title: Ionis’ antisense drug stabilizes ultra-rare disease ahead of FDA decision
snippet: The pivotal study of zilganersen in Alexander disease missed a secondary endpoint, but analysts expect the FDA to approve the asset given the unmet need and overall data.

title: Lilly exits Rigel alliance, adding to RIPK1 scrap heap
snippet: Eli Lilly and Rigel Pharmaceuticals partnered in February 2021 to advance a pair of RIPK1 blockers, but the pharma in October last year pulled the plug on one of these programs for central nervous system indications.

title: Trump’s psychedelics push, Lilly’s up to $7B CAR T deal, Replimune’s saga and Denali’s big win

title: Lilly, Novo dip as proposed Medicare coverage for GLP-1 pilot thrown off balance
snippet: Two of the biggest insurance providers have expressed reluctance to participate in the government’s BALANCE program that would have made GLP-1 drugs more affordable to patients.

title: Amid US ordeal, Moderna wins EU approval for flu/COVID-19 combo shot
snippet: In the U.S., Moderna withdrew its approval application for the combination vaccine in May last year and the timeline for resubmission remains uncertain.

title: Merck steps up as 'meaningful competitor' to Gilead with HIV pill approval
snippet: The newly approved HIV drug Idvynso will also help Merck diversify as loss of exclusivity looms over its top-selling product, the mega-blockbuster cancer drug Keytruda.

title: The 5 largest IPOs in biopharma history
snippet: With an IPO raise of $625 million, Kailera Therapeutics now holds the new record for the largest public market debut.

title: The next phase of diabetes care
snippet: As technology becomes more integrated and personalized, experts say the biggest opportunity in diabetes management is reducing everyday burdens.

title: Denali CEO’s ‘greatest professional moment’ arrives as rare disease drug launches
snippet: After receiving the FDA’s greenlight for Hunter syndrome drug Avlayah, Denali Therapeutics CEO Ryan Watts saw the culmination of 20 years of hard work unraveling the mysteries of the blood-brain barrier.

title: Why key opinion leader enthusiasm often doesn’t predict future commercial success
snippet: While prominent physicians can provide companies with valuable guidance during development, their perspective is limited when it comes to projecting how well or how readily a new product will be adopted. Here’s how to perform rigorous commercial diligence.
